# TypeScript Template

This is a TypeScript project template using webpack for Spck for NodeJS. This project template was made with the help of `createapp.dev`.

## Building and Running

Pressing the ▶ button will call the command `build` in `package.json`. If `dist/bundle.js` file does not exist, it indicates this may be the first run and `install-dep` in `package.json` will be called. The `spck.config.json` file controls which command to call when pressing ▶ (which can be modified in **Run Settings**).

The task `build` creates a development build of the project and generates:

- `dist/bundle.js`

When `build` finishes, the preview window will launch.

## Limitations in Android

Due to security restrictions in Android, execute permissions on write-allowed storage is likely forbidden on most stock devices. This prevents some npm scripts from working properly as `npm run` rely on the use of `sh` which requires exec permissions.

The `node` program is also built as a shared library for compatibility with future versions of Android and can only be accessed from the terminal and not `sh`.

For these reasons, using `npm run ...` will not work from the terminal, but entering the command (`webpack`) directly in the terminal will work.

## NPM Install

On external storage and SD cards, it is commonly using FAT32 or exFAT filesystems. These filesystems do not support symbolic links which is why npm dependencies that uses symlinks (mostly npm dependencies with command line usage symlinks) will fail on external storage.

Add the `--no-bin-links` option to `npm install` to prevent creation of symlinks.

```bash
npm i @babel/preset-env --no-bin-links
```

TypeScript 5.2 · Webpack 5 · ts-loader

Types that catch bugs before they catch you.

Edit src/index.ts and watch ts-loader hand webpack a fully type-checked bundle. The demos below tour the parts of TS that pay rent: generics, narrowing, unions, and utility types.

TS docs →
generics

One function, many shapes

Generics let a function preserve its input type all the way through. The return of last([1,2,3]) is number, not unknown. 

function last<T>(arr: T[]): T | undefined {
  return arr[arr.length - 1];
}
Idle.
discriminated unions

State machines, exhaustively

Tag your union members with a literal and TS will force you to handle every case. Forget one and the compiler shouts. 

type Result =
  | { kind: 'ok';    value: string }
  | { kind: 'error'; reason: string };
Idle.
narrowing

Smarter inference inside if

Inside a typeof guard, TS narrows the type automatically. No casts, no any. 

function describe(x: string | number) {
  return typeof x === 'string'
    ? x.toUpperCase()
    : x.toFixed(2);
}
Idle.
utility types

Reshape, don’t duplicate

Partial, Pick, Omit, Record, Readonly — you almost never need to write a new interface from scratch. 

type DraftUser = Partial<User>;
type UserPreview = Pick<User, 'id' | 'name'>;
Idle.
immutability

Lock state down

Mark inputs readonly and TS rejects mutation attempts at compile time — no runtime cost. 

function sum(xs: readonly number[]) {
  // xs.push(0)  ← compile error
  return xs.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
}
Idle.
branded types

Don’t confuse your IDs

A UserId and a PostId are both strings — until you brand them. Then the compiler stops you from swapping them. 

type UserId = string & { __brand: 'UserId' };
type PostId = string & { __brand: 'PostId' };
Idle.

tsc · output

    Edit src/index.ts · types live in src/types.ts · configured by tsconfig.json.

{
    "compilerOptions": {
        "outDir": "./dist/",
        "sourceMap": true,
        "strict": true,
        "noImplicitReturns": true,
        "noImplicitAny": true,
        "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
        "module": "es2020",
        "moduleResolution": "node",
        "target": "es2017",
        "lib": ["es2020", "dom"],
        "allowJs": true,
        "esModuleInterop": true,
        "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true
    },
    "include": [
        "./src/**/*"
    ]
}

.cache/
coverage/
dist/*
!dist/index.html
node_modules/
cdn_modules/
*.log
